pattern: Count subsets with a knapsack-style dynamic program over members used and profit accumulated, clamping profit at the threshold so every state at or above it shares one bucket, accumulating counts modulo the fixed prime.
hints: This is a counting knapsack: each crime is either included or skipped, and you want how many inclusion choices satisfy both a capacity and a floor.
Track states by members already committed and profit reached, but cap the profit dimension at the required minimum since any surplus is equivalent.

Process crimes one at a time, and for each, fold in the option of committing it when enough members remain; sum the counts at full-or-fewer members and at-or-above the profit floor, taking the modulus at every addition.

edge_inputs.description: zero minimum profit counts the empty scheme too
single member cannot afford a two-member crime
zero-profit crimes never reach a positive floor alone
exactly enough members for one crime

stress.description: small instances so the subset-enumeration oracle stays fast
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 12)))
    n = rng.randint(1, 12)
    minProfit = rng.randint(0, 8)
    group = [rng.randint(1, 6) for _ in range(m)]
    profit = [rng.randint(0, 6) for _ in range(m)]
    return [n, minProfit, group, profit]
stress.note: crime count bounded so the brute oracle's 2^m enumeration is cheap
